evidence_summary: [CH001_SC002] Purpose: Build tension and transition from peaceful prospecting to imminent danger.

[CH001_SC002] Summary: After Powell departs, Carter observes suspicious movements in the valley. He tracks what he believes is an Apache ambush, discovering fresh pony tracks that indicate a large presence nearby.
[CH001_SC002] Emotional arc: From solitude/peace to alertness and growing dread.
[CH001_SC002] Coverage: POV shots of the valley; low-angle tracking shots of Carter on horseback; close-ups of tracks in the dirt.
[CH001_SC002] Continuity: Horse movement/direction; lighting changes as time passes during the pursuit.

[CH001] Chapter summary: **Setting:** The Arizona hills and mountains; specifically a remote gold-bearing quartz vein, a high plateau filled with Apache tepees, and a large, ancient cave located on a narrow trail between a cliff and a ravine....
